chat.title: Agent Conversation Hub
chat.subtitle: Treat Dclaw Client as the main entry point for local work. Start with natural language, then move into runtime, automation, or reporting only when you need deeper control.
chat.empty.title: Start your local agent flow with one sentence
chat.empty.description: This page is becoming the real home of Dclaw Client. Ask the agent to inspect the workspace, draft a weekly report, explain the current runtime, or suggest the next best action.
chat.composer.label: Enter your request or question
chat.composer.placeholder: For example: inspect the current workspace first, then tell me the three most worthwhile things to move forward today.

chat.session.title: Session Controls
chat.session.description: Keep the homepage simple and only expose the controls that matter most for conversation.

chat.prompt.weekly: Draft a weekly-report outline from the work currently in progress.
chat.prompt.automation: Show me which repeated local tasks are strong candidates for automation.
chat.prompt.nextStep: If I only do one important thing today, what should it be?
chat.guidance.title: How to use it
chat.guidance.pointOne: Start here with natural language, then move into runtime or automation when you need a more explicit workflow.
chat.guidance.pointTwo: If agent chat is unavailable, check the OpenClaw bridge page and confirm the CLI connection first.
chat.guidance.pointThree: This area is the natural place to add Agent Hub, Skill Store, and future task distribution entry points.

reports.title: Git Weekly / Monthly Report
reports.subtitle: Scan a workspace for repositories or target one repository directly, then generate a markdown work report.
reports.businessContextPlaceholder: Optional context, project goals, audience, or reporting constraints.
reports.currentTemplatePending: Will resolve after base report generation
reports.prompt.rawReportIntro: The following is the raw work report generated from git history. Reorganize it strictly from this content and do not invent accomplishments that are not present:
reports.prompt.weekly.instructions:
Please rewrite the following Git work report into a Chinese weekly report suitable for internal team syncs.
The output must be Chinese markdown. Do not output YAML, JSON, fenced code blocks, or explanatory prefixes.
The title must include the exact date range.
If the material is insufficient, explicitly write "待补充" instead of inventing content.
Prioritize completed work this week, key repository progress, risks, blockers, and next week plans.
Recommended structure:
1. Weekly overview
2. Completed items
3. Major project / repository progress
4. Risks and blockers
5. Next week plan
6. Collaboration / support needed
reports.prompt.monthly.instructions:
Please rewrite the following Git work report into a Chinese monthly report suitable for internal team syncs or updates to a manager.
The output must be Chinese markdown. Do not output YAML, JSON, fenced code blocks, or explanatory prefixes.
The title must include the exact date range.
If the material is insufficient, explicitly write "待补充" instead of inventing content.
Prioritize deliverables, major repository progress, risks, and next month plans.
Recommended structure:
1. Monthly overview
2. Key achievements
3. Major project / repository progress
4. Risks and issues
5. Next month plan
6. Collaboration / decision support needed
reports.prompt.leadership.instructions:
Please rewrite the following Git work report into a Chinese executive summary for management.
The output must be Chinese markdown. Do not output YAML, JSON, fenced code blocks, or explanatory prefixes.
The title must include the exact date range.
Focus on outcomes, impact, delivery rhythm, risks, and the next stage plan, while reducing low-level technical detail.
If the material is insufficient, explicitly write "待补充" instead of inventing content.
Recommended structure:
1. Core conclusions for this period
2. Key completed items
3. Business / team impact
4. Current risks and dependencies
5. Next stage plan
reports.empty.markdown: Generated markdown report will appear here.
reports.empty.aiMarkdown: OpenClaw-generated Chinese report will appear here.

shell.app.prompt.mobileOffice: Please help me design a mobile remote-work flow that includes file lookup, message handoff, and risk reminders.
shell.app.prompt.mailWorkflow: Help me design a mail send/receive and unified mailbox management workflow, and tell me how it could become a skill later.
shell.sidebar.emptyState: No conversation history to show yet. Click “New Conversation” above to get started.
shell.sidebar.updateTitle: The new shell is still being refined
shell.sidebar.updateDescription: This desktop shell already supports Chinese and English. Agent Hub, the Skill marketplace, and real task scheduling are next.
shell.sidebar.inspiration.label: Inspiration
shell.sidebar.schedule.label: Scheduled Tasks
shell.home.emptyTitle: What should Dclaw help you with today?
shell.home.emptyDescription: Start from a conversation, then branch into files, reports, scheduled tasks, and agent orchestration as the workflow becomes clearer.
shell.home.activeDescription: Last updated at {time}. You can keep asking follow-up questions, add more context, or move into the studio for concrete automation work.
shell.home.heroTitle: Bring conversation, files, reports, tasks, and agent orchestration into one desktop workspace
shell.home.heroDescription: This iteration brings internationalization back while tightening the product layout, so the homepage can handle both chat-first workflows and the entrances to deeper tools.
shell.home.quickPrompt.one: Check my current workspace first and tell me what needs attention most.
shell.home.quickPrompt.two: Generate a draft weekly report from my recent work progress.
shell.home.quickPrompt.three: Help me design a desktop assistant workflow that can handle files, spreadsheets, and scheduled tasks.
shell.home.step.one.title: 1. Start with one sentence
shell.home.step.one.description: Tell Dclaw the goal, such as a report, file cleanup, or reminder flow, and it will help structure the request first.
shell.home.step.two.title: 2. Move to the studio when execution matters
shell.home.step.two.description: Task templates, folder processing, Excel merging, and report generation stay in the studio for focused execution.
shell.home.step.three.title: 3. Reuse inspiration or scheduling entry points
shell.home.step.three.description: The inspiration area is good for reusable prompts, while the schedule page is good for turning rough intent into repeatable tasks.
shell.home.bridgeHint.label: Connection Hint
shell.home.bridgeHint.readyTitle: You can start chatting right away
shell.home.bridgeHint.setupTitle: OpenClaw CLI setup is still recommended
shell.home.bridgeHint.readyDescription: The app can already send direct agent turns. If you later need AI reports or more agent routing, continue in the studio.
shell.home.bridgeHint.setupDescription: You can still type requests now, but the system will ask you to finish the OpenClaw setup in the studio before live agent execution works smoothly.
shell.home.composerPlaceholderReady: For example: summarize this week's progress and draft a weekly report
shell.home.composerPlaceholderCli: You can type the request now, but live agent turns require the OpenClaw CLI connection first

shell.inspiration.page.title: Turn common work scenarios into reusable capability entries
shell.inspiration.page.description: This space collects reusable scenarios. Open the details directly or send a prompt into the chat workspace to continue from there.
shell.inspiration.modal.usageDescription: Send this prompt directly into the conversation area and continue from there
shell.inspiration.banner.newsDigest.titl: Create a scheduled task: daily hot-news digest
shell.inspiration.banner.newsDigest.desc: Still scanning news one item at a time? Let the app compile the important pieces for you every day.
shell.inspiration.banner.travelPlan.desc: Not sure where to go this weekend? Let the ideas come to you directly.
shell.inspiration.item.hotNews.title: Create a scheduled task: daily hot-news digest
shell.inspiration.item.hotNews.descripti: Stop scanning headlines manually. Let the important AI and tech updates be condensed for you every morning.
shell.inspiration.item.hotNews.prompt: Create a daily 8:00 AM news digest task that collects important AI and technology headlines and sends me a concise summary.
shell.inspiration.item.hotNews.scenarioD: Useful for people who want to understand daily industry shifts quickly without reading every source by hand.
shell.inspiration.item.invoiceArchive.de: Month-end reimbursement chaos again? Let the messy invoice pile become a structured archive.
shell.inspiration.item.invoiceArchive.pr: Help me design a receipt-processing workflow that recognizes invoice images and PDFs, then archives them by month, vendor, and amount.
shell.inspiration.item.invoiceArchive.sc: Useful for people who frequently handle receipts, reimbursement materials, or finance-related attachments.
shell.inspiration.item.scheduleFollow.de: Too many tasks and not enough mental space? Let the system track timing, progress, and reminders for you.
shell.inspiration.item.scheduleFollow.pr: Create a schedule-tracking assistant that reminds me of important events every morning and checks completion status at 5 PM.
shell.inspiration.item.scheduleFollow.sc: Useful when meetings, follow-ups, and execution milestones need regular structure.
shell.inspiration.item.docCreate.title: One-click knowledge base setup
shell.inspiration.item.docCreate.descrip: Clip the web, aggregate documents, and turn scattered notes into a team-ready wiki structure.
shell.inspiration.item.docCreate.prompt: Organize the current workspace documents into a knowledge-base structure and tell me which key documents are still missing.
shell.inspiration.item.docCreate.scenari: Useful for early-stage teams that need to shape documentation and structure scattered knowledge quickly.
shell.inspiration.item.marketResearch.de: No direction for a new initiative yet? Turn market noise into a structured research brief.
shell.inspiration.item.marketResearch.pr: Help me build a market-research task template that organizes hot sectors, competitors, risks, and opportunities automatically.
shell.inspiration.item.marketResearch.sc: Useful for idea validation, product scoping, and opportunity analysis.
shell.inspiration.item.foodRecommend.des: Set the taste preference and get a health-conscious meal path that still feels enjoyable.
shell.inspiration.item.foodRecommend.pro: I want a healthy dinner that still feels satisfying tonight. Recommend a meal plan and the kind of nearby places I should look for.
shell.inspiration.item.foodRecommend.sce: Useful for anyone who wants to balance convenience, taste, and health.
shell.inspiration.item.fitnessAdjust.des: Has the routine fallen apart? Use the assistant to rebuild a plan that is easier to keep.
shell.inspiration.item.fitnessAdjust.pro: Build a weekly workout plan that is easier to sustain, with lighter sessions on weekdays and longer exercise on weekends.
shell.inspiration.item.fitnessAdjust.sce: Useful for people who want to rebalance work rhythm and personal health routines.
shell.schedule.page.title: Turn one sentence into a repeatable task arrangement
shell.schedule.page.description: This version still starts from natural language: describe what should run on a schedule, and Dclaw will help turn it into a more complete task definition.
shell.schedule.page.placeholder: For example: every day at 8:00 AM, summarize the latest AI news for me in English
shell.schedule.page.helper: For now the request is routed into the conversation area first. A real scheduler and execution engine comes next.
shell.schedule.suggestion.astrology: Create a daily 8:00 AM scheduled task that sends me today's action guidance based on my zodiac sign, Gemini.
shell.schedule.suggestion.water: Create a water reminder that runs every weekday at 10:00 AM, starts today, stays active continuously, and is enabled immediately after creation.
shell.schedule.suggestion.techNews: Send me the latest 10 tech news items every day, each summarized very briefly.

shell.settings.about.positioning.title: Product positioning
shell.settings.about.positioning.body: Dclaw Client is moving toward a desktop agent orchestration center. This version unifies chat, the studio, inspiration, and scheduled-task entry points first, with Agent Hub and the Skill marketplace coming next.
shell.settings.about.next.title: Next priorities
shell.settings.about.next.one: 1. Turn the chat experience into the true product homepage and agent entry point.
shell.settings.about.next.two: 2. Upgrade scheduled tasks from intent capture into visual orchestration and execution.
shell.settings.about.next.three: 3. Connect the client with the hub service for installation, ratings, comments, and private sharing.
shell.seed.skillhub.title: Check whether SkillHub is already installed
shell.seed.skillhub.userPrompt: Please check whether the SkillHub CLI is already installed and tell me how to search for and install skills.
shell.seed.skillhub.assistantReply:
# SkillHub Store

- CLI path: `C:\Users\Administrator\.local\bin\skillhub`
- Search command: `skillhub search <keyword>`
- Install command: `skillhub install <skill-name>`

## summarize skill

- Location: `C:\Users\Administrator\.openclaw\workspace\skills\summarize`
- Purpose: quickly summarize URLs, local files, PDFs, images, and videos.
shell.seed.wechat.title: Abnormal WeChat support messages
shell.seed.wechat.userPrompt: Help me organize the recent WeChat support messages that need priority replies and suggest a handling approach.
shell.seed.wechat.assistantReply:
I would split the messages into three groups first:

1. Urgent complaints: respond manually within 30 minutes.
2. Order tracking: confirm the order ID and logistics status first.
3. Product questions: convert them into FAQ material and later into quick-reply templates.
shell.seed.github.title: What fun GitHub projects are worth watching recently?
shell.seed.github.userPrompt: Recommend a few GitHub projects worth tracking lately, ideally around automation and AI tools.
shell.seed.github.assistantReply:
Three areas are worth watching first:

- Local agents and desktop copilots
- Document, spreadsheet, and file automation
- Prompt tooling and skill distribution platforms

If you want, I can next narrow them down specifically from the angle of what Dclaw could borrow.
shell.conversation.system.cliRequired: Live conversation currently requires OpenClaw CLI mode. Please finish the setup in the OpenClaw panel inside the studio first.
shell.conversation.system.emptyReply: The agent did not return any displayable text.
